title: General — Memorandum Overview
source_text: 10-1 General. A memorandum provides a less formal way to correspond within an activity/command. Subordinates within that activity or command may use a memorandum to correspond directly with each other on routine official business or as an informal means of communication.
plain_language_rule: A memorandum is a less formal correspondence method used within an activity or command. Subordinates within the same activity/command may use memorandums to correspond directly with each other on routine official business or as an informal means of communication.
renderer_impact: This rule selects memorandum handling for less-formal correspondence within an activity/command. It does not prescribe a specific memorandum layout; layout rules are defined in subsequent Chapter 10 sections.
notes: Source text resolved from SECNAV M-5216.5 CH-1, 10-1 General. Key points: (1) memorandums are less formal than letters, (2) used within an activity/command, (3) subordinates may correspond directly with each other, (4)适用于 routine official business or informal communication. Enforceability is partial because determining organizational scope (same activity/command) requires external context.
